source_text: Implementations that receive a KeyUpdate message prior to receiving a Finished message MUST terminate the connection with an "unexpected_message" alert.
rule_statement: If KeyUpdate is received before Finished, the implementation MUST terminate with unexpected_message.
applies_when: Endpoint receives KeyUpdate before handshake Finished
checkability_rationale: Can verify implementation rejects KeyUpdate before handshake complete.

source_text: After sending a KeyUpdate message, the sender SHALL send all its traffic using the next generation of keys, computed as described in Section 7.2.
rule_statement: After sending KeyUpdate, the sender SHALL use next-generation keys for all subsequent traffic.
expected_value: all subsequent traffic uses new keys
checkability_rationale: Can verify sender uses new keys after KeyUpdate.

source_text: Upon receiving a KeyUpdate, the receiver MUST update its receiving keys.
rule_statement: Upon receiving KeyUpdate, the receiver MUST update its receiving keys.
expected_value: receiving keys updated to next generation
checkability_rationale: Can verify receiver can decrypt with new keys after KeyUpdate.

source_text: If an implementation receives any other value, it MUST terminate the connection with an "illegal_parameter" alert.
rule_statement: If request_update field contains an invalid value (not 0 or 1), the receiver MUST terminate with illegal_parameter.
applies_when: Endpoint receives KeyUpdate with invalid request_update value
checkability_rationale: Can verify correct alert on invalid request_update value.

source_text: If the request_update field is set to "update_requested", then the receiver MUST send a KeyUpdate of its own with request_update set to "update_not_requested" prior to sending its next Application Data record.
rule_statement: If request_update is update_requested, the receiver MUST send its own KeyUpdate with update_not_requested before next Application Data.
applies_when: Endpoint receives KeyUpdate with request_update = update_requested
expected_value: respond with KeyUpdate(update_not_requested) before next app data
checkability_rationale: Can verify response KeyUpdate is sent before next application data.

source_text: Both sender and receiver MUST encrypt their KeyUpdate messages with the old keys.
rule_statement: KeyUpdate messages MUST be encrypted with the old (current) keys, not the new keys.
checkability_rationale: Can verify KeyUpdate is decryptable with current keys.

source_text: Additionally, both sides MUST enforce that a KeyUpdate with the old key is received before accepting any messages encrypted with the new key.
rule_statement: Both sides MUST enforce that KeyUpdate (encrypted with old key) is received before accepting messages with new key.
expected_value: KeyUpdate received before new-key messages accepted
checkability_rationale: Can verify endpoint rejects new-key messages without prior KeyUpdate.
ambiguity_notes: Failure to enforce allows message truncation attacks.
